text.title: iac-patterns-module-design Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for iac-patterns-module-design. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Base Module: manages exactly one resource type with sensible defaults. Use for S3 buckets, security groups, IAM roles. Exposes minimal inputs; all security defaults locked.
rule.rationale: Single-resource modules are the atomic unit of reuse — testable in isolation, versioned independently, composable into higher-level patterns.

rule.statement: Wrapper Module: wraps a community or base module with non-overridable org-specific defaults (encryption always on, never publicly accessible, backup retention enforced for prod). Use to enforce compliance without repeating policy across teams.
rule.rationale: Centralizing security defaults in a wrapper means each consumer gets policy compliance for free; the wrapper module is the single point to update when policy changes.

rule.statement: Facade Module: composes multiple internal modules into a simplified interface. Consumers provide only high-level inputs (project, environment, container_image); all inter-module wiring is internal. Use for complete stack deployments.
rule.rationale: Facades reduce the consumer's cognitive load from dozens of required inputs to a handful, while preserving flexibility through sensible defaults on everything else.

rule.statement: Factory Module: creates multiple similar resources from a configuration map using for_each. Accepts a map of objects, one entry per resource; outputs a parallel map. Use when teams define 3+ similar resources (S3 buckets, security groups, DNS records).
rule.rationale: for_each on a typed map is safer than count because resource identity is keyed by name, not index — adding or removing items doesn't shift all subsequent resources.

rule.statement: Composite Module: orchestrates two or more sub-modules into a reusable higher-level unit without becoming a Facade (it exposes full outputs of all components). Use for VPC+subnets+NAT, or EKS+node-groups+IRSA.
rule.rationale: Composite keeps co-deployed resources versioned together while still exposing all outputs for dependent modules — a middle ground between raw composition and a Facade's hidden internals.

rule.statement: Keep modules focused and single-purpose. A module that creates "everything my app needs" is an antipattern — split by resource type or concern.
rule.rationale: Single-purpose modules are independently testable and versionable; monolithic modules create tight coupling and uncontrollable blast radius.

rule.statement: Use semantic versioning (v1.0.0). Pin module calls to a version constraint. Bump major version for breaking changes; provide a migration guide.
rule.rationale: Without versioned modules, downstream consumers silently receive breaking changes on the next init.

rule.statement: Pass dependencies into modules as inputs (dependency inversion). Never use data sources inside a module to look up resources that the caller already owns.
rule.rationale: Modules with internal data source lookups are untestable without real cloud state; input-based dependencies enable plan-time testing with mocks.

rule.statement: Set sensible defaults for all optional variables. Required variables MUST have validation rules with descriptive error messages.
rule.rationale: Defaults reduce the consumer's required inputs; validation fails fast with a readable message rather than an opaque API error during apply.

rule.statement: Expose minimal but sufficient outputs. Every output MUST have a description. Sensitive outputs MUST be marked sensitive = true.
rule.rationale: Minimal outputs reduce the surface area for accidental secret leakage; descriptions are required for auto-generated documentation tools (terraform-docs).
